raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The section makes non-trivial consistency claims — 'eventual consistency in the absence of partitions' (unusual framing), bounded staleness formalized as max(2T, 50ms), and availability preservation across 3-region partitions — each of which requires logical scrutiny for internal coherence and soundness; central under a technical-correctness reading.",
    "R1.3": "Specific quantitative results (p99 read latency 8.4ms / 142ms, 50k writes/sec load, 47-minute partition simulation) are asserted inline without referencing a figure or table in this section; checking that claims are grounded in reported evidence is expected in any substantive review.",
    "R1.6": "A bare 'Review this section' delegation plausibly targets prose quality: the section opens with a redundant restatement of the challenge, mixes motivation and results within the same paragraphs, and uses hedged phrasing ('no client-visible inconsistencies observed') that may understate or obscure the guarantee being claimed."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the review prioritize technical correctness — verifying the logical validity of the consistency/staleness claims and whether experimental results support them — or editorial quality — improving prose clarity, flow, and paragraph structure?"
}

rationale.R1.2: The section makes non-trivial consistency claims — 'eventual consistency in the absence of partitions' (unusual framing), bounded staleness formalized as max(2T, 50ms), and availability preservation across 3-region partitions — each of which requires logical scrutiny for internal coherence and soundness; central under a technical-correctness reading.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative results (p99 read latency 8.4ms / 142ms, 50k writes/sec load, 47-minute partition simulation) are asserted inline without referencing a figure or table in this section; checking that claims are grounded in reported evidence is expected in any substantive review.
rationale.R1.6: A bare 'Review this section' delegation plausibly targets prose quality: the section opens with a redundant restatement of the challenge, mixes motivation and results within the same paragraphs, and uses hedged phrasing ('no client-visible inconsistencies observed') that may understate or obscure the guarantee being claimed.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review prioritize technical correctness — verifying the logical validity of the consistency/staleness claims and whether experimental results support them — or editorial quality — improving prose clarity, flow, and paragraph structure?
